subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Main Board & Cards
- **1** game board (village on left, raiding locations on right)
- **1** offering tile bag
- **32** townsfolk cards (crew members)
- **7** Valkyrie tokens
- **18** offering tiles (in cloth bag)

#### Resources & Tokens
- **Silver tokens** — currency for hiring crew
- **Gold tokens** — worth VP at end of game
- **Iron tokens** — needed for raids
- **Provisions tokens** — needed for raids
- **Livestock tokens** — needed for certain raids

#### Plunder & Score
- **Plunder tiles** — earned from successful raids
- **Score markers** — 1 per player on VP track
- **1** black worker (starts on board)
- **Player workers** — white workers (1 per player to start)

#### Player Components
- **Player mats** — not used; board is shared
- **Reference cards** — 1 per player

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place the board** — Central area visible to all players.
2. **Village (left side)** — Contains work spaces: Silversmith, Armoury, Provisions, Barracks, Treasury, Town Hall, and more.
3. **Raid locations (right side)** — Harbours, Outposts, Monasteries, Fortresses arranged top to bottom by difficulty.
4. **Place the black worker** — Put the single black worker on the designated starting space.
5. **Fill offering bag** — Place all offering tiles in the cloth bag and mix.
6. **Place Valkyrie tokens** — Set Valkyrie tokens near the Valkyrie track.
7. **Plunder tiles** — Place plunder tiles face-down on raid locations as indicated.
8. **Townsfolk deck** — Shuffle all townsfolk cards and place face-down as a draw pile. Reveal a row of available crew.

#### Raid Location Requirements
- **Harbours** — Easiest raids, require fewer crew and resources.
- **Outposts** — Medium difficulty raids.
- **Monasteries** — Hard raids, require more crew and provisions.
- **Fortresses** — Hardest raids, require largest crews and iron.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Receives
- **1 white worker** — your starting worker
- **1 score marker** — place on 0 of VP track
- **Starting silver** — receive a small amount of silver (varies by turn order)

#### Starting Crew
- Deal starting townsfolk cards to each player as indicated in the rulebook (typically 3-5 cards depending on player count).

#### Turn Order
- Determine a starting player randomly. Play proceeds clockwise.
- Later players receive slightly more starting resources to compensate.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — chosen randomly, takes the first turn.
- **Black worker placement** — The black worker starts on one of the village spaces. On your turn you will interact with it.
- **Game flow** — The game has no fixed rounds. Players take turns until enough Valkyrie or plunder spaces are depleted to trigger the endgame.

#### Important Starting Notes
- You always start with a **white worker** in hand.
- The **black worker** is already on the board from setup.
- Your first turn follows the same place-one-take-one structure as every other turn.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each turn consists of exactly **two steps**:

#### Step 1: Place a Worker
- Take the worker from your hand and place it on an **empty** space on the board.
- Perform the **action** of that space.

#### Step 2: Pick Up a Worker
- Pick up a **different** worker from any occupied space on the board.
- Perform the **action** of that space a second time.

#### Worker Colors Matter
- **Village spaces** (left side) — generally require placing a **white** worker to use, and you pick up the **black** worker (or vice versa).
- **Raid spaces** (right side) — require placing the **black** worker to initiate a raid.
- After a raid, you pick up a white worker from the village to continue the cycle.

This place-one-take-one mechanic means you always perform **two actions** per turn.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Village Actions
- **Silversmith** — Gain silver.
- **Armoury** — Gain iron.
- **Provisions** — Gain provisions.
- **Barracks** — Hire a crew member from the available row by paying their silver cost.
- **Treasury** — Gain gold (worth VP).
- **Town Hall** — Recruit townsfolk for special abilities.
- **Long House** — Draw townsfolk cards.
- **Gate House** — Gain provisions and prepare for raids.

#### Raiding
- To raid, you must **place the black worker** on a raid location.
- You must meet the **crew requirement** (number and type of crew cards) and **resource requirement** (provisions, iron, livestock).
- Discard the required crew cards and resources.
- Gain the **plunder tile** from that location.
- Advance on the **Valkyrie track** if crew members are lost (some crew die in raids).
- Gain **offerings** — draw tiles from the offering bag.

#### Crew Cards
- Each townsfolk card shows a **crew strength**, **special ability**, and **hiring cost**.
- Some crew are military (sword icon), some are spiritual (cross icon).
- Military crew are needed for fortress raids; spiritual crew help with monastery raids.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Valkyrie Track
- When crew members **die** during a raid (marked with Valkyrie symbol), advance your marker on the Valkyrie track.
- Higher positions on the Valkyrie track score significant VP at game end.
- Some crew are more expendable and intentionally have Valkyrie icons.

#### Offering Track
- After successful raids, you draw offering tiles from the bag.
- Offerings come in different types and score points for **sets** at end of game.
- Collecting diverse offerings is very valuable.

#### Armour
- Certain plunder tiles and crew provide **armour** bonuses.
- Armour scores VP at end of game based on total collected.

#### Gold
- Gold tokens are worth VP at the end of the game.
- Gained from treasury actions and some raid rewards.

#### Hired Crew Abilities
- Many crew cards have ongoing or one-time abilities that trigger when hired or during raids.
- Building a synergistic crew is key to efficient raiding.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Triggering the End
The game ends when **one of these conditions** is met:
- All **offering tiles** in the central supply are taken.
- Enough **Valkyrie tokens** have been claimed.
- All raid spots of a sufficient number have been completed.

When triggered, finish the current round so all players have equal turns.

#### Final Scoring
Add up VP from all sources:
- **Plunder tiles** — VP printed on collected plunder.
- **Valkyrie track** — VP based on position (higher = more points, up to ~16 VP).
- **Offerings** — VP for sets of different offering types (larger sets score exponentially more).
- **Gold** — Each gold token is worth 1 VP.
- **Armour** — VP based on total armour icons collected.
- **Crew bonuses** — Some crew cards provide end-game VP.

#### Tiebreaker
If tied, the player with more remaining silver wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Hire crew early** — You need crew to raid, so spend your first few turns recruiting strong crew members.
- **Gather provisions** — Provisions are the most universally needed resource for raids.
- **Target harbours first** — Harbour raids are cheapest and give you early plunder and offerings.
- **Don't ignore iron** — You'll need iron for the more valuable outpost and fortress raids later.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Hoarding resources** — Don't stockpile without raiding. Resources don't score; plunder does.
- **Ignoring the Valkyrie track** — The Valkyrie track is a major VP source. Sending crew to die in raids is intentional.
- **Neglecting offerings** — Sets of offerings score very well. Aim for diversity.
- **Forgetting the worker swap** — Remember you always place one worker AND pick up another. Plan both actions together.
- **Late raiding** — If you wait too long to raid, the best locations and offerings may be gone.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Which crew to hire** — Balance between crew with Valkyrie icons (for track advancement) and crew with strong abilities.
- **When to transition from village to raiding** — Early raiding gets easier targets; late raiding gets bigger rewards but higher costs.
- **Offering diversity vs. speed** — More diverse offering sets score better, but each raid takes time.
- **Gold vs. resources** — Gold is safe VP but raids provide more total scoring potential.
- **Which raid tier to target** — Fortresses give the most plunder but require the biggest investment.
